node.title: Is 3 month notice period a deal breaker?

article.title: Is 3 month notice period a deal breaker?
article.content:
Hi, recently my company suddenly updated the probabtion period guidelines, cancelling the probabtion of certain level and making them permenant. Although it was a good thing but on the downside thee notice period has now become 3 months which was supposed to b 1 month for first 6 months.

Are companies allowed to do that even if it was signed in the offer letter?
Based on todays market, what all companies will be accepting this 3 month NP? Anyone with similar experience?

post.content: You\'re probably talking about US/India, right? In EU 3 month probation is typical, I\'ve never encountered a problem with that.

I doubt that the probation period was in the offer letter. More likely, it referred to a general working conditions document. But obviously if you have a contract in hand that says something else and it doesn\'t contain anything that the employer can modify certain terms, then you can just reply that you want to continue with the terms in the contract. It takes both parties to change a contract.

Totally understand the concern sudden policy changes can feel unfair even if they look \u201Cpositive\u201D on paper. A 3-month notice is still accepted by a lot of fintech, SaaS, and even big MNCs, but startups usually prefer shorter NPs. Just make sure HR provides the updated terms officially.
